title: Knol Ven'nari
content:
{{Top|fa|leg}} {{Character |type=Jedi |image=[[File:Knolvennari.jpg]] |name=Knol Ven'nari |homeworld= |birth= |death=[[22 BBY/Legends|21.7 BBY]], [[Queyta]][[Republic 53|''Republic'' 53]] established that Knol Ven'nari [[Death/Legends|died]] on [[Queyta]] during the [[Mission to Queyta]], an event that ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates to [[22 BBY/Legends|21.7 BBY]]. |species=[[Bothan/Legends|Bothan]]{{FactFile|111|FAC 1-6|The New Face of War}} |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Female]] |pronouns=She/her[[Republic 53|''Republic'' 53]] |height= |mass= |hair=[[Color/Legends|Brown]] |feathers= |eyes=[[Color/Legends|Green]] |skin= |cyber= |families= |parents= |partners= |siblings= |children= |affiliation=*[[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] *[[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|''Master Knol Ven'nari''… The Bothans called her 'Fire Eater'… She once fought Wookiee bandits to save all of Bothan space.|Obi-Wan Kenobi, regarding Ven'nari|}} '''Knol Ven'nari''' was a [[Bothan/Legends|Bothan]] [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] who lived during the final [[Republic Classic era|decades]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. She was nicknamed the '''Fire Eater''' by her fellow Bothans for her ability to [[Pyrokinesis|manipulate]] [[Fire/Legends|fire]] with [[The Force/Legends|the Force]]. Ven'nari eventually was elevated to the rank of Master, although she refused to take a [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]]. Instead, she chose to wander [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] and go wherever the Force guided her, rarely visiting the [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Jedi Temple]] on the galactic [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. Ven'nari grew to be regarded as a legend of the Jedi Order. During her time as a [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]], Ven'nari saved all of [[Bothan Space/Legends|Bothan space]] by repelling [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] bandits. In [[22 BBY/Legends|21.7 BBY]], four [[Standard month|months]] after the [[First Battle of Geonosis/Legends|initiation]] of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], Ven'nari accompanied fellow Jedi Masters [[Jon Antilles]], [[Nico Diath]], [[Fay]], and [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] on a [[Mission to Queyta|mission]] to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Queyta]]. Ven'nari and the team were assigned to infiltrate a mobile [[Separatist Queyta base|Separatist facility]] that rode on one of Queyta's [[Magma/Legends|lava]] streams and acquire a sample of the [[Antidote/Legends|antidote]] for [[swamp gas]], a [[Bioweapon/Legends|chemical weapon]] that the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Separatists]] planned to use against the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic's]] [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone troopers]]. Shortly after Ven'nari and the other Jedi infiltrated the base, they confronted the Separatist agents [[Asajj Ventress/Legends|Asajj Ventress]] and [[Durge/Legends|Durge]]. During the encounter, Durge shot two [[Skakoan/Legends|Skakoan]] sentries, causing them to explode. Ven'nari [[Force power/Legends|used]] the Force to draw the flames toward herself, suffering severe burns in the process. Diath suggested evacuating her for treatment, but she declined, stating that her time had come. Diath then used the Force to ease her pain as she [[Death/Legends|succumbed]] to her injuries. Of all the Jedi on the mission, only Kenobi survived, successfully recovering the antidote and returning to the Jedi Temple. A [[Hologram/Legends|holographic]] memorial was held at the [[Jedi temple/Legends|Jedi Temple]] to honor Ven'nari and the other fallen Masters. ==Biography== ===Early years=== {{Quote|They were the nomads of the Jedi Order, rarely coming to Coruscant, but fighting for peace and freedom across the stars.|Obi-Wan Kenobi, regarding Ven'nari and the other Masters|}} [[File:KnolVennariHologram-Republic53.jpg|thumb|right|160px|Ven'nari was considered to be a legend in the Jedi Order.]] Knol Ven'nari, a [[Bothan/Legends|Bothan]] [[Gender/Legends|female]], was active during the final [[Republic Classic era|decades]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. She was taken in by the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] and [[Jedi training/Legends|trained]] in the ways of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]], eventually achieving the rank of [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]]. Ven'nari refused to train a [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]], however, choosing instead to roam [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] and go wherever the Force guided her.{{CSWECite|III|302|Ven'nari, Knol}} During her time as a [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]], Ven'nari fought [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] bandits in order to save [[Bothan Space/Legends|Bothan space]]. Her fellow Bothans nicknamed the Jedi Master "Fire Eater" for her ability to [[Pyrokinesis|manipulate]] [[Fire/Legends|fire]] by using the Force. She rarely visited the [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Jedi Temple]] on the galactic [[Capital/Legends|capital]] of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. By the time of the [[First Battle of Geonosis/Legends|initiation]] of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], which was the Galactic Republic's war with the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]], Ven'nari was regarded as one of the legends of the Jedi Order. ===Mission to Queyta=== ====The strike team==== {{Quote|Let's get this over with—I'm needed elsewhere, and the chemical factory is already on the move.|Knol Ven'nari|}} [[File:Queyta.jpg|thumb|left|300px|Ven'nari took part in a mission to Queyta.]] In [[22 BBY/Legends|21.7 BBY]], four [[Standard month|months]] into the Clone Wars, Ven'nari [[Mission to Queyta|traveled]] to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Queyta]] and met with Jedi Masters [[Jon Antilles]], [[Nico Diath]], and [[Fay]] in order to recover a sample of the [[Antidote/Legends|antidote]] for the Separatists' [[Bioweapon/Legends|chemical weapon]] known as [[swamp gas]]. The Separatists had successfully [[Battle of Ohma-D'un|tested]] the airborne gas against the [[Gungan/Legends|Gungan]] colony on [[Ohma-D'un/Legends|Ohma-D'un]], a [[Moon/Legends|moon]] of the planet [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]]. With this bioweapon, the Separatists could decimate the [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone troopers]] who served the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]] with no harm to their own [[Battle droid/Legends|droid]] armies.[[Republic 51|''Republic'' 51]] When [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] rendezvoused with the Jedi Masters, Ven'nari expressed her impatience to Kenobi regarding his arrival. She also questioned Antilles's involvement in the mission due to the fact that he was not assigned to their team. After conversing with Antilles, Ven'nari and the Masters successfully infiltrated the mobile Separatist [[Separatist Queyta base|base]], which was located on a small island moving through the planet's [[Magma/Legends|lava]] stream. Once the Jedi were inside, both Ven'nari and Diath noted that the base was not guarded by battle [[Droid/Legends|droids]]. The Bothan remarked that the facility was manned and determined that the guards would be easy to deal with. However, Antilles noted that the guards were [[Skakoan/Legends|Skakoans]]—a [[Species/Legends|species]] that breathed [[Methane/Legends|methane]] and was forced to wear pressurized [[Armor/Legends|armor]]—and that attacking them with a [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] would cause them to explode. ====Fighting the Separatists==== {{Quote|No, the burns are too extensive… my time here is over.|Knol Ven'nari's last words|}} [[File:KnolVennariDeath-Repulic53.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Ven'nari dies after sacrificing herself to save her fellow Jedi's lives.]] Ven'nari and the Jedi confronted the [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] [[Asajj Ventress/Legends|Asajj Ventress]] and the [[Gen'Dai/Legends|Gen'Dai]] [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Durge/Legends|Durge]], who notified the team of Jedi that their [[Spy/Legends|spies]] had already informed them of the group's presence in the facility. As Durge shot two of the Skakoans, Ven'nari implored the strike team to stand aside. When the Skakoans exploded, Ven'nari used her pyrokinetic abilities with the Force to pull the fire toward her. After the fire subsided, Diath and the team found Ven'nari with extensive burns, and she told Diath she was about to [[Death/Legends|die]]. Diath used the Force to ease Ven'nari's suffering as she passed away. After her death, Diath, Antilles, and Fay were also killed. However, Kenobi successfully recovered the antidote and returned to the [[Jedi temple/Legends|Jedi Temple]] on Coruscant. Once Kenobi had recovered from his wounds, the Jedi held a [[Hologram/Legends|holographic]] memorial service in honor of Ven'nari and the other deceased Jedi. ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|''Finally''. You Coruscant Jedi sure take your time, don't you?|Ven'nari, to Obi-Wan Kenobi|}} Knol Ven'nari was regarded as one of the nomads of the Jedi Order, rarely visiting the Jedi Temple on Coruscant. Nevertheless, she still fought for peace and freedom in the [[Galaxy/Legends|galaxy]]. Even after being promoted to the rank of Master, Ven'nari refused to take a Padawan. She was resentful of Jedi stationed on Coruscant, and was eager to complete the mission on Queyta so that she could then attend to business elsewhere. She also openly questioned Jon Antilles's involvement in the mission, going so far as to call him a drifter. Ven'nari was willing to sacrifice herself to ensure the safety of her fellow Jedi. Ven'nari had long, flowing [[Color/Legends|brown]] [[Hair/Legends|hair]] and green [[Eye/Legends|eyes]]. ==Powers and abilities== {{Quote|It's stopped? Are you holding the fire back?''"
"''No! Master Ven'nari is pulling it in!''"
"''The 'Fire Eater'…of course!|Obi-Wan Kenobi and Nico Diath, regarding Ven'nari's ability to manipulate fire with the Force|}} Ven'nari was skilled enough to achieve the rank of Jedi Master. She was capable of using the Force to [[Pyrokinesis|manipulate fire]], which earned her the nickname "Fire Eater." During the mission to Queyta, Ven'nari also demonstrated her ability with [[Telekinesis/Legends|Force flight]], using this to infiltrate the Separatist facility. She also used the last dregs of her power to [[Control Pain|ease her suffering]] as she died. ==Equipment== Ven'nari wore a set of brown [[Jedi apparel/Legends|Jedi robes]] and used a lightsaber containing a green [[Lightsaber crystal/Legends|crystal]]. ==Behind the scenes== Knol Ven'nari first appeared in flashbacks in the [[Republic 53|fifty-third issue]] of the ''[[Star Wars: Republic]]'' comic series. The issue was written by [[W. Haden Blackman]], illustrated by [[Brian Ching]], and published by [[Dark Horse Comics]] on [[April 30]], [[2003]].{{Marvel|url=comics/issue/54501/star_wars_republic_2002_53|text=Star Wars: Republic (2002) #53}} Her name was misspelled as "Knol Ven'Nari" in the [[2006]] reference book ''[[Star Wars: The Comics Companion]]'', written by [[Ryder Windham]] and [[Daniel Wallace]].''[[Star Wars: The Comics Companion]]'' ==Appearances== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Knol Ven'nari}} *[[Republic 53|''Republic'' 53]] {{1st}} {{Flash}} ==Sources== *{{FactFile|102|KEN 25-28|Obi-Wan Kenobi}} {{Imo}} *{{FactFile|111|FAC 1-6|The New Face of War}} *{{FactFile|118|MAC 17-18|Mace Windu}} *{{FactFile|128|FAY 1-2|Master Fay}} {{Imo}} *''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' {{C|''Mission to Queyta''}} {{Imo}} *''[[Star Wars: The Comics Companion]]'' {{C|''Blast Radius''; misspelled as "Knol Ven'Nari"}} *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Knol Ven'nari |es=Knol Ven'nari |nl=Knol Ven'nari |pl=Knol Ven'nari }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ven'nari, Knol}} [[Category:Bothans]] [[Category:Females]] [[Category:Individuals with she/her pronouns]] [[Category:Post-Ruusan Jedi Masters]]
